1_background.title: Background / Context
1_background.subtitle: Why does this project exist? Who is affected?
1_background.content:
Patry's property management division handles ~50 tenant maintenance requests per week across our portfolio.

Requests arrive via email in free-text form — tenants describe issues in their own words, often with incomplete information.

This process directly impacts tenant satisfaction, contractor response times, and the property management team's capacity.

This project supports Patry's Summer 2026 AI mandate to deliver seven-figure measurable value across all divisions.

2_current_condition.title: Current Condition
2_current_condition.subtitle: How does the process work today? Use data, not opinions.
2_current_condition.content:
Current workflow (fully manual):
1. Staff member reads each email
2. Manually categorizes by urgency (Emergency / Standard) and trade type
3. Looks up the correct contractor in a directory spreadsheet
4. Sends a follow-up email to the assigned contractor
5. Logs the outcome in a tracking spreadsheet

Data:
• 6–8 hours/week of staff time on this process
• ~50 requests/week across 2.5 weeks of intake data (51 rows)
• 10 contractors across 5 trade types
• Known issues: missed requests, delayed assignments, duplicate submissions, inconsistent categorization

3_goal.title: Goal / Target Condition
3_goal.subtitle: What does 'done' look like? Be specific and measurable.
3_goal.content:
Reduce manual processing time from 6–8 hours/week to under 30 minutes/week by automating the full triage pipeline.

Target: 90%+ of requests classified, matched, and logged without human intervention.

Timeline: Working prototype by end of Week 2, validated against all 51 rows of historical data.

Success metric: Staff time saved per week, measured by comparing pre/post hours logged on this task.

4_root_cause.title: Root Cause Analysis
4_root_cause.subtitle: Why does the problem exist? Ask 'why' five times.
4_root_cause.content:
Why does triage take 6–8 hours?
→ Because every request is read and categorized manually

Why is it done manually?
→ Because urgency and trade type aren't structured — they're buried in free-text descriptions

Why is the data unstructured?
→ Because tenants submit via email with no standardized form

Why hasn't this been automated before?
→ Because extracting urgency/trade from natural language required AI capabilities that weren't previously accessible

Root cause: The core bottleneck is the classification step — converting unstructured tenant language into structured urgency + trade type categories. Everything downstream (matching, notification, logging) is mechanical once classification is done.

5_countermeasures.title: Countermeasures / Proposed Solution
5_countermeasures.subtitle: What are you building? How does it address the root cause?
5_countermeasures.content:
An n8n workflow that automates the full pipeline end-to-end:

1. READ — Pull incoming requests from the Maintenance Inbox (Google Sheets)
2. CLASSIFY — Use Claude API (HTTP Request node) to extract urgency, trade type, and reasoning from free-text
3. MATCH — Look up the correct contractor from the directory based on trade + urgency tier
4. NOTIFY — Construct a notification payload for the assigned contractor
5. LOG — Write every outcome (completed, skipped, errored) to the Output Log

This directly addresses the root cause by replacing manual classification with AI-powered extraction.

Edge case handling: duplicates detected and flagged, missing data logged as errors, non-English requests processed.

6_implementation.title: Implementation Plan
6_implementation.subtitle: Milestones, owners, and dates. Who does what by when?
6_implementation.content:
Week 1:
• Day 1–2: Set up n8n environment, connect to data sheets
• Day 3–4: Build and test HTTP Request node + Claude API integration
• Day 5: Parse API response, build contractor matching logic

Week 2:
• Day 1–2: Add error handling, duplicate detection, edge case branches
• Day 3: Run full workflow against all 51 rows, validate output
• Day 4: Record video walkthrough, write ROI note
• Day 5: Submit all 4 components

Dependencies: Anthropic API key (provided), Google account for output sheet, n8n cloud trial

7_followup.title: Follow-Up & Verification
7_followup.subtitle: Did it work? What did you learn? What's next?
7_followup.content:
Metrics to track:
• Classification accuracy (% matching manual review)
• Processing time per request (target: seconds vs. minutes)
• Error rate on edge cases

Before: 6–8 hrs/week manual processing
After: [To be measured post-implementation]

Next steps if successful:
• Deploy on live email intake (not just spreadsheet)
• Add Slack/email notifications to contractors
• Expand to other request types across Patry's portfolio
